page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-60 is a memory-optimized server instance within the c3d family. It features 60 vCPUs mapped to 30 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor using the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 480.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance across compute and memory-intensive operations. The AMD EPYC processor achieves top 25% scores in both single-core and multi-core stress-ng evaluations, alongside strong multi-core Geekbench and Passmark CPU scores. Memory performance is highlighted by a strong Passmark memory score and top-tier read-only cached small-block memory bandwidth. However, uncached large-block read and write memory bandwidths fall within the average range. The instance also exhibits strong performance in database operations, Redis SET operations, and software compilation, while static web serving throughput and bzip3 decompression remain average.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c3d-highmem-60 offers high resource density for memory-bound workloads without the added expense of bundled local storage or GPU accelerators. The primary tradeoff is the reliance on network-attached storage, as local NVMe drives are absent. This hardware profile is highly suited for memory-intensive applications such as large-scale database operations, Redis caching layers, software compilation, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks where high memory capacity and strong multi-core processing are required.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-60 is a memory-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor with 60 vCPUs and 480.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB of memory per vCPU. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server features 30 physical cores with hyper-threading but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks place this instance in the top 25% for multi-core CPU execution, Passmark database operations, and Redis SET throughput, though uncached large-block memory bandwidth and static web serving remain average. Qualitatively, it offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-heavy workloads by omitting expensive local NVMe and GPU hardware. The c3d-highmem-60 is highly suited for large databases, in-memory caching, software compilation, and medium-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-60 is a memory-optimized server featuring 60 vCPUs, 480 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC processor. It delivers strong multi-core and database performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-60 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 60 vCPUs and 480 GB RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized server featuring sixty AMD EPYC vCPUs and strong database performance for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9B14 processor with 60 vCPUs and 30 physical cores.
Equipped with 480 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio.
Delivers strong performance in database operations, Redis SET, and software compilation.
Lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, optimizing cost for pure memory workloads.
Best suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and medium-scale LLM inference.
